item.title: Ostium Halts Trading After Oracle Exploit Drains up to $18M from Vault
item.description: Blockaid says an attacker used a registered price-feed forwarder and future-dated oracle reports to book fake trading profits, in the latest exploit to target the automated infrastructure DeFi protocols lean on for pricing.

item.title: RT by @Dinosn: A single prompt got an AI to hand over S3 bucket contents, and Amazon Bedrock's content moderation never flagged it. We broke down the full exploit chain, the sanitized PoC, and the fix
item.description: pA single prompt got an AI to hand over S3 bucket contents, and Amazon Bedrock's content moderation never flagged it. We broke down the full exploit chain, the sanitized PoC, and the fixes in this week's Exploits Explained: a href="https://hubs.ly/Q04pFqJS0"hubs.ly/Q04pFqJS0/abr /br /a href="https:/

item.title: Claude Flaw Automatically Sends Malicious Prompts to AI Agents
item.description: When combined with another exploit, the &quot;PromptFiction&quot; vulnerability, which has been fixed, could have enabled an end-to-end attack on a targeted system.

item.title: RWA Perpetual Protocol Ostium Suffers Suspected $18 Million Exploit on Arbitrum Security firm Blockaid said it detected an exploit involving Ostium Vault on Arbitrum. According to Blockaid, the attack
item.description: pRWA Perpetual Protocol Ostium Suffers Suspected $18 Million Exploit on Arbitrum br /br /Security firm Blockaid said it detected an exploit involving Ostium Vault on Arbitrum. item.title: 2-Click Cursor Exploit Enables Dev Environment Takeover
item.description: Simple age-old bugs give bad actors access to developers' secrets and source code-rich environments.
